meaning: adj.不让进入,不会受到损伤的,密封的,不受影响的
examples: She seems almost impervious to the criticism from all sides.
This also makes the painting impervious to water.
It dispels the notion that the plants are impervious.
They fit so tightly together they are nearly impervious to rain and sun, outlasting all other colors.
The old lady stormed over to see her husband but found him impervious to her outrage.
